content:
# Agentic Retrieval Protocol — Architecture Decision (2026-03-27)

## Decision
Server-side intelligence over agent instructions. Complex retrieval logic (quality assessment, gap detection, query reformulation hints, write dedup) lives in corvia's MCP tools. Agent instructions are minimal (~20 lines in AGENTS.md).

## Why
- LLM self-assessment is ~70% reliable (KDD 2025 survey)
- Explicit gap reporting has <20% compliance across memory frameworks (Mem0, Letta, Zep research)
- More AGENTS.md instructions past a threshold = lower compliance
- Simple unconditional rules get followed consistently across Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, Copilot

## Key Patterns
1. Search responses include `confidence` + `suggestion` fields — agent follows suggestion, doesn't reason about quality
2. Context injection via `corvia_context` with token budget param — universal subagent pattern
3. Implicit gap detection from search logs — no agent action required
4. Implicit feedback via access tracking (issue #12) — no explicit ratings
5. Server-side write dedup (similarity > 0.9) — prevents bloat without agent burden

## Dropped
- Time-based write triggers (not implementable)
- Write reminders / server push (MCP is request-response)
- Explicit result ratings (unreliable compliance)

## Issue
chunzhe10/corvia#47
